source: Learning bash with Rocky - Tests: Testing variables/strings/integers, combined tests, and arithmetic

questions.q: Which option tests whether a variable is empty?
Which operator tests two strings for equality?
questions.why: [[ str1 = str2 ]]; != is inequality; < and > compare ASCII order.

questions.q: Which operator tests two integers for equality?
questions.why: -eq equal, -ne not equal, -gt greater than, -lt less than.

questions.q: What is the difference between `test "1" = "1"` and `test "1" -eq "1"`?
options: = compares them as strings (ASCII); -eq compares them as numbers
questions.why: Same result here, but = means equal ASCII characters while -eq means equal numeric values.

questions.q: With the internal command, how do you AND two tests together?
questions.why: && / || inside [[ ]]; -a (AND) and -o (OR) with the external test; ! negates.

questions.q: Which command performs arithmetic with integers (spaces required around the operator)?
questions.why: expr 2 + 2 -> 4; the * for multiply must be escaped as \*.

options: Both -- let var=5+5 and $((5+2)) evaluate math; typeset -i also makes a var integer
questions.why: let and $(( )) evaluate arithmetic; typeset -i declares a variable as an integer so assignments are computed.
